dialog_header_classification: Classification attributes are global admin objects that must be unique in a tenant and should be reused in several classes whenever it is possible to limit their numbers. Once all classification attributes are created, request the platform adminstrator to refesh the index model and reload the cache and wait for his feedback before using them.
dialog_header_ipx: Export control attributes are global admin objects that must be unique in a tenant and should be reused in several classes whenever it is possible to limit their numbers. Once all Export control attributes are created, request the platform adminstrator to refesh the index model and reload the cache and wait for his feedback before using them.
title: Title
authorizedValues_placeholder_int: Enter numeric values with only [0-9] characters
minMax_placeholder_int: Enter a numeric value with only [0-9] characters
defaultValue_placeholder_real: Enter or select a numeric value (e.g., 1 or 1.5)
defaultValue_placeholder_int: Enter or select a numeric value with only [0-9] characters
defaultValue_placeholder: Enter or select a default value
defaultValue_placeholder_attr_dim: Enter a default value in SI unit
invalid_internalName: Special characters and spaces are not supported
invalid_authorizedValues: Special characters and spaces are not supported
invalid_defaultValue: Special characters and spaces are not supported
invalid_msg_int_real: Letters and special characters are not allowed
outofrange_defaultValue: This value should be in range
type_short_tooltip_ipx: String is the default attribute type.
type_short_tooltip_classification: Select the attribute type (string, integer, real, boolean, date, or attribute with dimension).
internalName_short_tooltip: Enter a unique name that starts with a letter, and do not use any special characters. Only [a-z], [A-Z], [0-9], and underscores are supported.
internalName_long_tooltip: When you create an attribute, the entered name is automatically appended with the suffix
predicateValue_short_tooltip: Search and select a predicate from the list. You cannot change the predicate once the attribute is created.
predicateValue_long_tooltip: Once you select a predicate, the Title field is disabled. Choose a custom predicate for each attribute and do not use existing predicates. Your administrator can create custom predicates and import them using the a vocabulary RDF file.
displayIn6WTagger_short_tooltip: This option is turned off by default. You cannot enable this field until you select a predicate.
displayIn6WTagger_long_tooltip: Turn on this option to expose the newly created attributes in the Tagger.
title_short_tooltip: If the predicate is already selected, it automatically appears as a title to ensure consistency or you can specify a new title.
title_long_tooltip: Choose a unique title for each attribute. Click Translate and enter the title in different languages. These translations can be added and modified even after attribute creation.
multiValuated_short_tooltip: Turn on this option to create an attribute that can have multiple value at the same time.
multiValuated_long_tooltip: You cannot assign a default value for a multivalued attribute.
multiLine_short_tooltip: Turn on this option to enter a long string value for the attribute. The entered value is displayed across multiple lines.
authorizedValues_short_tooltip: Only [a-z], [A-Z], and [0-9] characters are supported and do not insert spaces between values. Underscore is supported only for the String type attributes.
authorizedValues_long_tooltip: Use semicolon or press Enter to separate the authorized values. If you do not assign any authorized values, you cannot assign them later. Click Translate to add values in different languages, including special characters and spaces. These translations can be added and modified even after attribute creation.
defaultValue_short_tooltip: Select or enter a default value for the attribute.
defaultValue_long_tooltip: The default value is mandatory if the Has Default Value option is turned on. If authorized values are absent, you can still set a default value or leave it blank. For attributes with dimensions, you must enter values in SI units.
hasADefaultValue_short_tooltip: Turn off the Has Default Value option if there is no default value. By default, it is on.
hasADefaultValue_long_tooltip: Turning on the Multivalued option disables the Has Default Value option, and vice versa. If you create an attribute with Has Default Value turned off, it cannot be changed.
isEditable_short_tooltip: Turn on this option to create attributes whose values can be edited. By default, it is turned off and attributes are read only.
minimumValue_short_tooltip: Enter the minimum value that an attribute can have.
minimumValue_long_tooltip: A minimum and maximum value must always be defined together, and entering either disables the Authorized Value field. Additionally, If you entering Authorized Value it disables the Minimum Value and Maximum Value fields.
maximumValue_short_tooltip: Enter the maximum value that an attribute can have.
maximumValue_long_tooltip: A minimum and maximum value must always be defined together, and entering either disables the Authorized Value field. Additionally, If you entering Authorized Value it disables the Minimum Value and Maximum Value fields.
dimension_long_tooltip: Once the attribute is created, you cannot change the dimension later.
displayUnit_short_tooltip: Select the required unit to display the attribute values.
displayUnit_long_tooltip: The entered value gets automatically converted in the display unit unless the Display as Entered option is enabled. Once the attribute is created, you cannot change the display unit later.
displayAsEntered_short_tooltip: Enable this option to display attribute values and units as entered, without conversion.
displayAsEntered_long_tooltip: This option can be enabled or disabled even after attribute creation.
confirmation_info: After creation, you cannot change certain properties such as Name, Predicate, minimum and maximum values, and other related properties depending on the attribute type.
vlaue_used: This value is already in use
